unassigned_stints.reason: No agreeing dimension beyond the fuzzy surname block ('ABERY' vs 'Abey'). The biography is a medical officer ('Assistant colonel surgeon, Ceylon, 1867', honours M.B.C.M.); the stint is a 'Stationmaster' in the 'Tasmanian Government Railways' — different profession, different colony (Ceylon vs Tasmania), no shared honour, no edition co-occurrence (bio appears only in 1897 as a 46-year-old career note; the stint spans 1894-1917 in a wholly different staff list). The bio carries a single initial 'J' while the stint is 'J. R.', and single-initial biographies require a strong independent dimension, which is absent. A career starting in Ceylon medicine in 1867 followed by Tasmanian railway station work into 1917 (50 years later) is implausible as one person.

Same person as the Tasmania stint records (identical name 'J. R. Abey', identical position 'Stationmaster', identical department 'Tasmanian Government Railways'), so the same objections apply: the biography is a Ceylon medical officer of 1867 with honours M.B.C.M.; this stint is a Tasmanian railway stationmaster listed 1913-1918, 46+ years later, with no colony, position, honour, or edition agreement. All deterministic checks beyond the surname/initial gate FAIL. The single-initial trap forbids merging without a strong independent dimension, and none exists.

notes: The two stints ('Abey, J. R___Australia___1913' and 'Abey, J. R___Tasmania___1894') are almost certainly the same individual railway stationmaster split by a colony-labelling artifact (the 1913/1918 records list department 'Tasmanian Government Railways' under colony 'Australia', plausibly post-federation relabelling). That person is distinct from the biography subject Joseph Abery, M.B.C.M., a Ceylon medical officer of 1867. Surname match is fuzzy (ABERY vs Abey), profession and geography are disjoint, and the chronology (1867 appointment vs service to 1917-1918) strains a single-person reading. Recommend the two stints be linked to each other in any downstream stint-merging step, but not to this biography.
